alerts.title: Trump halts planned Iran strike; negotiations resume amid breakout window
alerts.summary: President Trump announced cancellation of scheduled Iran strike at request of Gulf allies (WXOW, WGEM, The Sumter Item), while simultaneously signaling decision could resume within 10 days (WBMA). Development occurs as Iran maintains 60% U-235 enrichment and ~1-2 week nuclear breakout estimate (global_metrics), and US presses missile program inclusion in Geneva talks (Al-Monitor). Hardened Iranian negotiating stance (rejecting missile program limits per previous alert) combined with imminent strike threat creates high-volatility diplomatic-military standoff.

alerts.title: Israeli strikes kill 3 in Lebanon amid ceasefire collapse, Iran response risk
alerts.summary: Israeli drone strikes in southern Lebanon and Beirut suburbs killed 2-3 civilians including conservationist Mona Khalil (NBC News, Al Jazeera, BBC, Anadolu Agency), occurring despite ceasefire framework and Washington negotiations. Hezbollah asserts violation; multiple strikes logged within 24hrs (Nabatieh area, 33.8-34.1°N, 35.6-35.9°E). Pattern indicates Israeli unilateral operations outside formal diplomatic constraints, raising risk of Iranian retaliation that could trigger wider escalation.

alerts.title: USS Florida submarine positioned in Persian Gulf amid Iran tensions
alerts.summary: USS Florida submarine deployed to Persian Gulf (25.97°N, 51.63°E per assets feed) amid critical threat level (9/10) and negotiations deadlock. Submarine positioning in chokepoint coincides with Iran's consolidation of Hormuz control via Iran-Oman joint fee-collection scheme (previous alert) and reports of ship explosions in strait (KATV citing Strait incident). Asset deployment signals US readiness for rapid response if nuclear talks collapse or Iranian provocation occurs.

alerts.title: Iran-linked cyber group claims FBI drone system breach, World Cup threat
alerts.summary: Iran-linked cyber group publicly claims successful penetration of FBI drone control systems and threatens World Cup event targets as retaliation for US Iran strikes (France 24, Middle East Eye per global_metrics cyber headlines). Claim reflects escalating Iranian cyber warfare doctrine and willingness to target critical US infrastructure and mass-casualty civilian events. If credible, represents expansion of Iranian asymmetric response toolkit beyond kinetic strikes.

alerts.title: US media sentiment shifts toward Trump court rulings, Iran coverage declines
alerts.summary: US sentiment data shows positive skew (54% positive vs 42% negative) dominated by Trump-related legal and administrative news (trump: 74 mentions, court: 28, supreme: 27), while Iran coverage drops to 15 mentions—tied with 'federal' and 'house' keywords. Shift reflects domestic political preoccupation and reduced media focus on Iran nuclear/military escalation narrative despite global_metrics threat level at critical (9/10). Sentiment decoupling from objective threat level may indicate reduced public/media pressure on administration Iran policy.

analysis_note: US-Iran negotiations at critical juncture with Trump simultaneously halting strikes and preserving strike option; Iranian nuclear breakout window narrows to 1-2 weeks while Israel escalates Lebanon operations risking Iranian retaliation; cyber and diplomatic dimensions expanding alongside kinetic threats.
